utterances.text: Not being critical of you all for that.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: I'm just saying that's the that's, you know, where it stands.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: And the other thing, I promise I'll wrap it up, is.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: Oh, the ability to raise funds, right?

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: So some of the Mitchell Llamas, the Upper West Side, they have commercial spaces underneath.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: They can charge rent for that.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: We have none of that.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: We have no, you know, we don't have a big parking lot that we can sell.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: We don't have these like other creative opportunities.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: And there's, so there's some differences between the Mitchell Lama co-ops.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: You know, that essentially allow other ones to raise funds.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: And, you know, we don't want to ask for handouts.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: We would love to be able to handle this internally ourselves, but the reality is we don't have the tools to do that.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: That's it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: There's something that I do notice that when buildings do leave the program,

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: If they don't have commercial space, they rebuild it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: They go in and they make adjustments so in the future they're going to bring themselves more money.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: I think as far as also looking at ways to save money, we need to look at ways that we can actually put...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: Commercial space in some of these buildings on the lower levels.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: Is there a way to upgrade it?

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: My mother's building left the program, and they redid, they took part of the lobby, the sidewalk, and they put big three, the whole avenue now is commercial space.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: We need to be able to look and do different things like that.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Janice Brody
utterances.text: And like the gentleman mentioned earlier, when y'all do your roundtable, you need to have some of us sitting there too.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Ryan Schallenberg
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: Heitler proposes scrapping the three-year rent increase system in favor of annual increases, faster loan processing, a small loan program for discrete repairs, mortgages for new purchasers, ending 2-to-11 conversions, and giving Mitchell-Lama rentals the option to convert to co-ops.
chapters.title: Richard Heitler, Village East Towers treasurer, testifies on annual rent increase proposal

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Oh, and I just want to say, I think,

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Oftentimes you hit the nail on the head that, because I'm a social worker and came from a nonprofit, so I often had to comply with a lot of the policies that the council members put in place.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So we hear you when you say that it's difficult to comply a lot of times, which is why I brought up the question about Local Law 97.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And I think that while well-intentioned, these are things that are going to impact folks in co-ops and condos like yours.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And as someone who, I say this to all the residents here, right, I have the most number of co-ops and condos in my district in eastern Queens.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: They're not much of llamas.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: They are housed with older adults, seniors on fixed incomes, union workers, teachers.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And so we need to do more because the housing was meant when it was first built for post-war veterans and folks coming back.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And we need to make sure that we're protecting that and not making you all have to pay on your backs to support a lot of the work that's going on.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So we appreciate you coming all the way to testify, and I appreciate everyone who's here.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: I just wanted to say that for the record because you're taking time out of your days to be here.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So thank you.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, next up we have Dominic, Dominic, oh, Dominic Pukumensa, Richard Heitler, and Valerie Brooks.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And please let her know she can still submit her testimony if she wants to.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, great.

chapters.summary: Heitler details how a three-year delay in closing on a capital improvement loan cost Village East Towers over $500,000 in additional pipe repairs, water tank replacement, and sidewalk shed costs, arguing delays are financially devastating.
chapters.title: Richard Heitler on loan delays costing Village East Towers over $500,000

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Press the button.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: First of all, I do want to thank you for the opportunity to testify, also to attend the hearing.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I come into this hearing feeling that I am speaking to friends and supporters.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We appreciate what you have done to get more funding allocated into the budget, and we appreciate your support for Mitchell Llama Housing.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: My name is Richard Heitler.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I have been a resident of Village East Towers on the Lower East Side since 1975.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I'm older than all of you.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And I've been on the board for 20 years, 18 of which is president.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I'm currently the treasurer.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I like to try to drive things to a number.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I'm also a founding member.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: member of Cooperators United for Mitchell-Lama, as is Valerie, as is Sally, as is Sam Moskowitz.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We already have a roundtable.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It's called the Board Members Roundtable, and it's people who are pro-Mitchell-Lama, got themselves elected to the board, and we share information and we do peer consulting about how we can do a better and better job.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Now, as you can see, I have a six-page testimony.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I will not even attempt to summarize it in two minutes.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I will hopefully say enough to at least, I don't know, encourage you to read it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: But I've been listening to the discussion and the debate back and forth.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: So I want to make some comments about testimony that I will have not submitted, but will submit within the next 72 hours.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We all recognize that rent increases are necessary.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We are all horrified when they are this extreme and this sudden.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And I want to say that I intend to, hi, HPD, I intend to take Adam's invitation.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: He said, if anybody has a proposal for an annual rent increase, I would take a look at it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I have it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It's nine pages long.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I've spent...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: The better part of eight months working on it and vetting it with various allies.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And I'll give you the

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Briefest summary, you got to scrap the whole complicated, unwieldy, unreadable three-year rent increase system.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: You ask for huge schedules that none of our shareholders can read.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And you produce an information when you finally get to the notice of approval, nobody understands what that means either.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Okay?

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: The system, I want to tell you what we're currently doing at Village East Towers and have for years, and the system that we propose.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: One, it must be annual.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: If you really are looking at our annual financials, you can see immediately if there's a gap.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It doesn't take a lot of math to fill it and figure out how much you need.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: You look at the operating deficit and the unpaid bills, and you come up with a proposal.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And for this reason, I think scrap the three years.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It's too complicated.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It's too cumbersome.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Rent increases have to be annual because nobody can actually anticipate what's happening three years down the road.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Secondly, the system of review and approval has to take place in a period of between not more than 90 or 120 days.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: At Village East Towers, we have information.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Well, first of all, I'm the treasurer.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I talk about finance.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: At every open board meeting, and we have them monthly.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And when our, can you give me a moment?

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Yes, finish, yes.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I will be as brief as I can, but I need you to reset the clock.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And it's the issue here is we have informational in-house meetings that are presided over by the board of directors and our accountant and our lawyer.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And we explain to every resident.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Exactly why the rent increases are needed and how much it's going to cost for their apartment.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: None of which is included in the notification letter that they will later get from HPD.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And then we're done.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And then we have to wait for HPD approval.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We had a deadline.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We're in the middle.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We're in the second phase of a...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Of a rent increase of five and five and five.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And it's higher than I would like, but it was needed.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It is the third rent increase that I put through during my term on the board.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We knew that our previous three-year rent increase expired.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And we needed a new one by November 1st.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We tried our hardest to do everything right.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We got final HPD approval to put the next rent increase in effect on September 1st of the following year.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: That gap blew it $200,000.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: hole in our budget.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And so my, if I have a theme, it is delays cost money

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: and we have to come up, we have to look at our system, see what's really necessary and what's not, and figure out how we can do it faster.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Six page, the small one, testimony that you have in front of you has five points.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: The first of which is faster processing for loans.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We have refinanced with HPD and HDC and we love you guys.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And the HPD people that I've worked with have been capable, dedicated, hardworking, and the system stinks.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We are one of those co-ops.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We're trying to figure out how you manage capital repairs.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: But we did an IPNA way back when, before anybody asked for it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We developed a scope of work, and we started working with HPD to develop a major capital improvements loan.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We have been working on that loan for three years, and we have not closed.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We have been told that we will close by the end of the month, and I hope that's real, because I've also calculated the cost of that delay.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Part of our IP&A included replacement of all the black pipe.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: The old brass plumbing, fabulous.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: But the drain pipes are cast iron and they leak.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And we have...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We have been trying to patch cast iron, and when they leak, they flood, and ceilings and walls are damaged.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We have, so the delay has cost us several hundred thousand dollars.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We knew from our IPNA that we had to replace the water tank.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It would not wait till loan closing.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We did it ourselves.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It was a hit on the operating budget.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: It should have been a capital expense in excess of $100,000.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We got one of our local law 11 buildings done.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We have two more to go.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We cannot afford to do, because those two things totally devastated our reserves.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We have to wait until we close the loan until we can do the local law 11 work.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: There's a kink in the code, although there is no danger of anything falling.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: If you have a condition that an engineer says requires repairs and you don't repair it in a year, then the...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: The sidewalk sheds have to go up.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Sidewalk sheds are costing us $25,000 a month.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: The delay is going to...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: You add all these three numbers together, and the failure...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We had a...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We did everything right.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We got an IPNA.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We had a scope of work.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We got the scope of work.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We got scope of work approved.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We got it bid out.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We had a successful bid in January of 2025.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And the fact that we have not been able to close on the loan has cost us more than half a million dollars.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: The other pieces of my six-page thing are five other suggestions for how to keep Mitchell Lama affordable, and they're nerdy, and they're small bore.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And that should be easy.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We also, in addition to the great big loan programs, we need a little loan program to repair vacant apartments.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: When an expensive piece of equipment goes down, it needs to be replaced.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We need a loan program that can close on a discrete scope for a small amount of money in 30 days or less.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: That's my second point.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: My third is we need mortgages for new purchases.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And if you want to speak, one of you guys talked about turnover and the questions of turnover takes a long time.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: A lot of it is the buyer has trouble raising the money and he can't get a mortgage.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And Sony may.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Will not do mortgages for Mitchell Llamas or limited equity co-ops.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I got two more.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: So if you could just quickly summarize.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: One sentence each.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Yeah, quickly summarize, yes.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: I'm with Gail Brewer.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: You have to end 2 to 11 conversions.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: They just make the co-op less affordable.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And I have my last one for my brothers and sisters in Mitchell Lama Rentals.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: each and every one of them.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: We're very pleased that everybody's interested in TOPA and COPA, and I hope it passes soon.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Don't forget the Mitchell Llamas.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Every Mitchell Llama rental should have the option to become a Mitchell Llama co-op and receive the technical support, legal support, training, and technical assistance needed to make a credible bid.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And thank you for your time.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: And any of your staff members who want to talk to me about any of these things,

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: You got my number on the sign-up sheet.

chapters.summary: Toro, a disabled retired Tracy Towers resident, explains he can't qualify for SNAP/SCRI due to his wife's income, fears losing his apartment, and argues HPD is a band-aid for a systemic problem requiring structural solutions.
chapters.title: Steven Toro, Tracy Towers resident, testifies on subsidy ineligibility and displacement risk

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.text: It's a very thoughtful suggestion, so we appreciate it.

utterances.text: Oh, you had to test it.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Oh, yeah.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, yeah, if you want to come to the front, and then we'll get your information afterwards.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: If you could just state your name and where you're from.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: My name is Steven Toro.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I live at Tracy Towers.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: And as we talked about before, our management brought in the city of New York, which we call HPD.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: HPD was trying to tell us that we could sign up for the SNAP program.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: It doesn't feasible for me to sign for a SNAP program because my wife still works, but I'm retired.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I'm over that $70,000 budget.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: When she files a tax return, I file with her.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: It puts me in a hole.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I can't afford to go back to work.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I'm disabled.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I'm going to lose my apartment because my wife can't make more money than what she's making now.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: So this band-aid that they offer us, I don't need it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I don't want it.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: Give it to somebody less fortunate, but help our building.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: That's all I ask.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I'm not begging for a handout.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I'm just saying work with us.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I remember when I was young that I worked with organizations to help bring back our community.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: I was known as a tenant organizer for Bronx Neighborhood Community Corporation.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: Phase one of the Bronx system, two, three, and four.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: We rebuilt all abandoned buildings.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: We took...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: unemployed workers from the street to rebuild these built-up buildings back in the 70s into the 80s.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: Why can't we do that now?

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: We don't need HPD.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: HPD is a band-aid.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: It's a systemic problem for our city.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: It should be taken out.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: That's all I have to say.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Steven Toro
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, so I don't know if there's anyone else seeing.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: I think that's everyone who's here in person.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: We're going to move to...

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Wait, what is this?

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Oh, okay, okay.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Got it.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: We're going to move over to Zoom, and I believe we have Sam Moskowitz.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Oh, wait, hold on.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: If there is, oh, yeah, this one.

chapters.summary: Moskowitz testifies about Gouverneur Gardens' $54M capital project and 70% low-income residents, recommending multi-year budget projections, simplified zero-sum budgeting, earlier owner's rep hiring, and tracking of vacancy and arrears as distress indicators.
chapters.title: Sam Moskowitz, Gouverneur Gardens board president, testifies on reform recommendations

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, so we're going to start with Sam Moskowitz.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And then I think there is someone, Jean Hall, I know was here in person, but I think there's someone else who may be using her account.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: So we'll start with Sam Moskowitz, followed by Jean Hall.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Can you hear me okay?

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Yes, we hear you.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Okay, I'm sorry I wasn't able to be there in person, but I've been listening to every word online from my office.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: My name is Sam Moskowitz.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: I'm the board president of Gouvernir Gardens, which is a 60-year-old, 782-unit Michelama co-op on the Lower East Side of Manhattan.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: I really appreciate everyone who's still there and everyone who showed up today.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: I know preserving affordable housing for hundreds of thousands of New Yorkers is not as sexy as banning horses.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: So I truly appreciate you all being here.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: I know for me personally, this is the most important hearing of the day, even though we won't be reading about it on the cover of the Post tomorrow.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: So our community at Gouvernir Gardens represents both the profound success of Michelama.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: But also shows the urgent need for structural reform.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Over 70% of our residents are low income, many of whom are seniors aging in place, and we're currently undergoing a $54 million capital improvement project to replace much of our aging infrastructure.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: After serving on the board for about 10 years and building relationships with other Michelamas, I'm very aware of the challenges we face.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: And I'm not here to complain, but I just wanted to come and give a list.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: of recommendations.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: So first, I won't dwell on the skyrocketing insurance costs because it's already been covered at length.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: I calculated our unit cost is about $1,800 per unit for insurance, which has doubled over the past few years.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: And so I just urge you to do whatever you can to further subsidize these costs and help us out.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: So we don't have to further pass on these costs and burden our low-income residents.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Second, which has also been touched on a lot today, is we really need to stop sacrificing the long-term stability of this program with short-term affordability.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: So for too long, the pattern has been don't raise carrying charges and deferred maintenance.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: The financial crisis facing us is not just a product of this recent inflation.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: It's a very...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Your time is expired.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Sorry.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Well, I did submit a full written testimony, and I have a number of recommendations, including

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: requiring multi-year budget projections, standardizing the reporting for one-, three-, and five-year budgets to transition developments from reactive crisis management to proactive planning.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Like Dick said, adopt simplified zero-sum budgeting, simplifying the process with HPD to avoid these disruptive double-digit spikes.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Assisting us by allowing us to hire a owner's rep earlier in the contract process for capital improvement.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Not doing so is a huge missed opportunity and costs our residents both financial resources and in their health.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: And finally, mandate the tracking of key indicators that would indicate financial distress, specifically vacancies and arrears.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: We budget for 5% vacancy loss, but it should be lower.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: But if there's co-ops that have higher losses, they need to be on some sort of corrective plan and helped by HPD in advance.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: So I did submit a much longer written testimony.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: Thank you all for your time.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Sam Moskowitz
utterances.text: And thanks again.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Great.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And next we have Jean Hall.

chapters.summary: Hall describes Rochdale Village's 25,000 residents facing a 31.2% carrying charge increase, aging infrastructure, and escalating costs. She recommends expanding city capital programs to state-supervised Mitchell-Lamas and creating a dedicated Mitchell-Lama capital fund.
chapters.title: Jean Hall, Rochdale Village board president, testifies on 31% carrying charge increase

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Good afternoon.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Trying to turn my camera on.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Good afternoon.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Thank you for taking the time.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Chair Lee and Chair Sanchez, members of the Finance and Housing Building Committee, thank you for this opportunity.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: My name is Jean Hall, and I am the board president of the Rochdale Village, Inc., a state-supervised mental health cooperative, which is located in Southeast Queens.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Rochdale Village is home to over 25,000 New Yorkers, including seniors, working families, and individuals living on a fixed or modest income.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: As one of the largest Mitchell-Lama cooperatives in the city, our long-term affordability is not only essential to our residents, it is essential to New York City's broader affordable housing landscape.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: And although we are a state-supervised housing company, the city requirements shape our financial reality every day, including Local Law 11, Local Law 97,

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: DOV elevator and modernization requirements, city water and sewer rate structures,

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: city property taxes, city capital and infrastructure programs, the city administered affordability tools such as J51, and the city council discretionary support for senior services and out-of-school.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Like many aging Mitchell Lama cooperatives, Rochdale Village is confronting aging infrastructure, escalating operation costs, rising insurance premiums,

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: elevator modernization needs, facade and roof repairs, utility and water costs that outpace our revenue and growth.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Without modern financial tools, cooperatives like ours are forced to depend on maintenance increases that strain the very residents Mitchell-Lama was created to protect.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Our 31.2 carrying charge in people affected November 2025.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Has pushed our community to the brink.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Respectfully, I'd like to recommend and support two things.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Expand city capital programs to improve...

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Your time has expired.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: ...Mitchell-Lamas and to create a dedicated Mitchell-Lama capital fund where we could get city capital funds

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: to help us address elevators, roofs, facade repairs, energy efficient upgrades, safety, and compliance.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: We look forward to working with New York City, Rochdale Village stands ready to work with the council, HPD, HCR, and all partners to secure the future of our community.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: And we will submit a substantial written report revised and is submitted now.

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Jean Hall
utterances.text: Thank you so much.

chapters.summary: Chair Lee confirms no further public testimony on Zoom or in person, reminds attendees of the 72-hour written testimony submission window, thanks the administration and public, and adjourns the hearing.
chapters.title: Hearing adjourned

utterances.speaker: Member of the Public - Richard Heitler
utterances.text: Thank you so much, Jean.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Okay, if there's anyone else on Zoom that would wish to testify and has not been called on, please use the raise hand function.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And if not, okay, seeing no hands.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Again, I'd like to note that everyone can submit written testimony to testimony at council.nyc.gov within 72 hours of this hearing.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And we thank the administration and the public for attending this hearing to share their thoughts on the oversight topic today.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And we look forward to following up on the issues.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: And with that, this hearing is now adjourned.

utterances.speaker: Linda Lee
utterances.text: Thank you.

summary: Oversight hearing on financial tools for maintaining Mitchell-Lama housing affordability, co-chaired by Finance Chair Linda Lee and Housing & Buildings Chair Pierina Sanchez
HPD officials testified on rising operating costs, capital needs, and tools like SCRI/DRI, shelter rent tax reductions, and J51 to mitigate rent increases across the Mitchell-Lama portfolio

Council members and residents raised concerns about steep rent increases (up to 30% at Tracy Towers), poor oversight of management companies, substandard repairs, and the need for proactive financial planning and a roundtable on Mitchell-Lama preservation
title: Committee on Finance
topic: Oversight - Financial Tools for Maintaining the Affordability of Mitchell-Lama Housing
